page: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server configured with an Intel x86_64 processor. The CPU allocation is shared, featuring 24 physical cores and 24 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. This processor is based on the Broadwell architecture, equipped with a 16 MB L3 cache, and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and FMA.

The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Graphics acceleration is delivered by four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, offering a combined total of 64 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In synthetic benchmarks, the server achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1766.38 and a multi-core score of 41473.88, both placing within the average tier. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-shared processing power, but the high density of GPU and memory resources provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing tasks.

This configuration is designed for workloads that benefit from multi-GPU acceleration and large memory footprints. Suitable use cases include virtual desktop infrastructure, video streaming, media transcoding, and machine learning inference tasks that utilize the Ampere architecture.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated cloud server featuring 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores on a shared Intel x86_64 Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 10.67 GB per core, alongside a 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Graphics processing is handled by four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with a total of 64 GB VRAM. In benchmark testing, the server recorded a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1766.38 and a multi-core score of 41473.88, both representing average performance. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense parallel workloads. The server is suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, media transcoding, video streaming, and machine learning inference workloads requiring substantial memory and multi-GPU capabilities.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram is a GPU-accelerated server with 24 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 64 GB VRAM, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-24c-256g-64vram features 24 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, four NVIDIA A16 GPUs, and 1200 GB NVMe storage for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server featuring four NVIDIA Ampere GPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and high-speed NVMe storage for parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM
24 vCPUs on a shared Intel x86_64 architecture
256.0 GB of system memory (10.67 GB per core)
1200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Optimized for virtual desktop infrastructure, media streaming, and inference
